setup: HSiKAN forward+backward (sign-routed Chebyshev splines + highway gate over signed hyperedges, arity 3, d=16, cheb 6, 2 branches), 100 iters. Nagare = closed-form no-tape, now EDGE-CHUNK PARALLEL (rayon, chunk_parallel_matches_serial gate). PyTorch = same architecture with autograd. Peak RSS via /usr/bin/time -l.
single_thread_note: Per-core (1 thread): Nagare 2.3x FASTER than PyTorch (24 vs 52 ms @20k, 62 vs 140 @50k, 124 vs 283 @100k). Memory 4-5x LESS (86 vs 459 MB, 207 vs 843, 405 vs 1468).
parallel_note: hsikan_forward/backward now partition the T hyperedges into min(threads,T) contiguous chunks run on the rayon pool; checksum identical across thread counts. At 16 threads Nagare scales 7.3x (61->8.3ms @50k) while PyTorch saturates ~6-8 threads (51->42ms). Matched 16 threads: Nagare ~5x FASTER + ~3x LESS memory. Parallelism costs P-fold transient grad_x copies: RSS 196->269 MB (50k), 386->568 MB (100k) from 1->16 threads -- still 2.6-3.1x under PyTorch.
